zh.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May是Johannes "Pancraz" May的兒子，於1425年出生在英國英格蘭戴文。他和Anna Katharina Dieter至少有1個女兒。他去世於1449年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May是Johannes "Pancraz" May的儿子，于1425年出生在英国英格兰戴文。他和Anna Katharina Dieter至少有1个女儿。他去世于1449年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Mal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slovenian: nickname for a physically small man, from mal ‘small, little’.
French (mainly Nord): nickname from mal ‘bad’ (from Latin malus).
Indian (northern states): variant of   Mall  .

variants.name: Mall
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Mall, a common form of Mald or Maud. See also Maude and Moll . It had several pet forms; see Malin , Malkin , and Mallet .
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit malla ‘strongman, wrestler’.

variants.name: Mali
meanings: Slovenian: nickname for a physically small man, from mali, the definite form of mal ‘small, little’ (see Mal 1). Compare Maley 3, Mallie 1, and Mally 1.
Albanian: from the personal name Mal (definite form Mali), from mal ‘mountain’, and probably also a topographic name based on this word (definite form mali).
Indian (mainly northern states): metonymic occupational name from Sanskrit mala ‘garland’. Traditionally, bearers of this name were growers of flowers and vegetables.

variants.name: Malic
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Malić); Slovenian and Czech (Malič): nickname for a physically small man, from South Slavic mal(i), Czech malý ‘small, little’, or from Serbian and Croatian malić, Slovenian malič ‘little one, dwarf’. The Slovenian surname may also be a patronymic from Mal 1 or Mali 1. Compare Malec , Malich , and Malick .
Americanized form of Czech and Slovak Malík, Polish and Slovenian Malik , cognates of 1.

variants.name: Malet
meanings: French: nickname from a diminutive of Mal ‘bad’ or from a derivative of the related Old French word maleit ‘accursed’ (see Mallet 1). Compare Malette .
French: from the Old French personal name Malet, a pet form of Malo (compare Mallet 2).
French: variant of   Mallet   3.

variants.name: Mallet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean-Yves, Adelard, Alain, Henri, Jacques, Leonce, Lucien, Monique, Philippe.

French: nickname from a diminutive of Mal ‘bad’. Alternatively, from a derivative of the related Old French word maleit ‘accursed’ (from Latin maledictus, the opposite of benedictus ‘blessed’), applied as a nickname for an unlucky person. Compare Malet 1.
French: variant of   Malet   2, an Old French pet form of the personal name   Malo  .

nameMeanings.name: May
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name May, a pet form of Matthew (see Mayhew ).

English, Dutch, and German: from a personal name or nickname taken from the month of May (Middle English, Old French mai, Middle High German meie, from Latin Maius (mensis), from Maia, a minor Roman goddess of fertility). This name was sometimes bestowed on someone born or baptized in the month of May; it was also used to refer to someone of a sunny disposition, or who had some anecdotal connection with the month of May, such as owing a feudal obligation then. In England, this name was possibly also a pet form of Mary or Margaret. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Denmark.
English: nickname from Middle English mey, may ‘kinsman’.

variants.name: Mae
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Akihiro, Ichiro, Kenichiro, Wakako, Yoshikazu.
Japanese (pronounced as two syllables): written 前 ‘front’ or ‘before’. It is not common in Japan. Some occurrences in America could be shortened versions of longer names beginning with this element.
Hawaiian: unexplained.

variants.name: Mayland
meanings: English (Yorkshire):

habitational name from any of the places so called in Essex and Durham. The Essex placename may derive from Old English mægthe ‘mayweed’ + land ‘land’ or from Old English ēg-land ‘island’ with initial m- carried over from a preceding thǣm (the dative case of the definite article). The Durham placename probably derives from the surname May + Middle English land ‘land’. There may have been some confusion with Malin .
variant of   Malin  .

variants.name: Maye
meanings: Irish: from Ó Miadhaigh ‘descendant of Miadhach’, a personal name meaning ‘honorable, proud’.
English: variant of   May  .

variants.name: Maike
meanings: German: from a pet form of   May  .
History: Carl Maike (1853–1937) emigrated from Grabrow (Grabroh), Germany, to Alma, KS, in 1881.

variants.name: Majewski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Casimir, Janusz, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Andrzej, Tadeusz, Jacek, Krzysztof, Mariusz, Stanislaw, Zofia, Bronislawa.
Polish and Jewish (from Poland): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Majewo.
Jewish (from Poland): surname adopted with reference to the month of May, Polish maj, assimilated to the Polish surname (see May ).

variants.name: Makin
meanings: English (northwestern): from the Middle English personal name Maykin, a diminutive of May , a pet form of Mayhew (see Matthew ). Compare Makinson .
English (northwestern): nickname from Middle English maide + diminutive suffix -kin ‘little maid’, perhaps used in a derogatory sense.
Irish: variant of   Mackin  .

variants.name: Maj
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Agnieszka, Jaroslaw, Wieslaw, Augustyn, Janusz, Jozef, Katarzyna, Krystyna, Krzysztof, Maciej, Piotr, Tadeusz.
Polish and Jewish (from Poland): surname adopted with reference to the month of May, Polish maj (see May 2). Compare May 9.
Jewish (from Poland): Polish-influenced variant of   May   8.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Mayes
meanings: English: variant of May 1, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Maio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Marco, Antonio, Carmela, Domenic, Rocco, Abilio, Attilio, Biagio, Carlo, Dante, Emilio, Francesco, Geremia, Gino, Rolando, Ruben.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from a personal name or nickname based on the word for the month of May, Latin Maius (see May 2).
Italian: possibly also from the personal name Maio of ancient Germanic origin (from magu- ‘boy’).

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Johannes .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Michael
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a common biblical name (meaning ‘who is like God?’ in Hebrew) borne by one of the archangels, the protector of the ancient Hebrews, who is also regarded as a saint of the Catholic Church. In the Middle Ages, Michael was regarded as captain of the heavenly host (see Revelation 12:7–9), symbol of the Church Militant, and patron of soldiers. He was often depicted bearing a flaming sword. The name is also borne by a Persian prince and ally of Belshazzar mentioned in the Book of Daniel. Since the early 1900s it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names in the English-speaking world.  See also  Michal .

en.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May was born in 1425, in Devon, England, United Kingdom as the son of Johannes "Pancraz" May. He had at least 1 daughter with Anna Katharina Dieter. He died after 1449.

fr.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May est né en 1425, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Johannes "Pancraz" May. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Anna Katharina Dieter. Il est décédé après 1449.

de.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May wurde 1425 in Devon, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Johannes "Pancraz" May geboren. Er hatte mit Anna Katharina Dieter mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1449.

it.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May è nato il nel 1425 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Johannes "Pancraz" May. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Anna Katharina Dieter. Morì dopo il 1449.

ru.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May родился в 1425 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Johannes "Pancraz" May. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Anna Katharina Dieter. Он умер после 1449 г.

es.lifeSummary: Johann Michael Mal May nació en 1425, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Johannes "Pancraz" May. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Anna Katharina Dieter. Murió después de 1449.
